english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Timothy

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus Apostolus Jesu Christi per voluntatem Dei, secundum promissionem vitæ, quæ est in Christo Jesu,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος ἀπόστολος ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ διὰ θελήματος θεοῦ κατ᾽ ἐπαγγελίαν ζωῆς τῆς ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of life which is in Christ Jesus,

verses.latin_vulgate: Timotheo carissimo filio: gratia, misericordia, pax a Deo Patre, et Christo Jesu Domino nostro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τιμοθέῳ ἀγαπητῷ τέκνῳ χάρις ἔλεος εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς καὶ χριστοῦ ἰησοῦ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: To Timothy, *my* dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, *and* peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratias ago Deo, cui servio a progenitoribus in conscientia pura, quod sine intermissione habeam tui memoriam in orationibus meis, nocte ac die
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χάριν ἔχω τῷ θεῷ ᾧ λατρεύω ἀπὸ προγόνων ἐν καθαρᾷ συνειδήσει ὡς ἀδιάλειπτον ἔχω τὴν περὶ σοῦ μνείαν ἐν ταῖς δεήσεσίν μου νυκτὸς καὶ ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: I thank God, whom I serve from *my* forefathers with pure conscience, that without ceasing I have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day;

verses.latin_vulgate: desiderans te videre, memor lacrimarum tuarum, ut gaudio implear,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπιποθῶν σε ἰδεῖν μεμνημένος σου τῶν δακρύων ἵνα χαρᾶς πληρωθῶ
verses.king_james: Greatly desiring to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy;

verses.latin_vulgate: recordationem accipiens ejus fidei, quæ est in te non ficta, quæ et habitavit primum in avia tua Loide, et matre tua Eunice, certus sum autem quod et in te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπόμνησιν λαμβάνων τῆς ἐν σοὶ ἀνυποκρίτου πίστεως ἥτις ἐνῴκησεν πρῶτον ἐν τῇ μάμμῃ σου λωΐδι καὶ τῇ μητρί σου ἐυνείκῃ πέπεισμαι δὲ ὅτι καὶ ἐν σοί
verses.king_james: When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice; and I am persuaded that in thee also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quam causam admoneo te ut resuscites gratiam Dei, quæ est in te per impositionem manuum mearum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δι᾽ ἣν αἰτίαν ἀναμιμνῄσκω σε ἀναζωπυρεῖν τὸ χάρισμα τοῦ θεοῦ ὅ ἐστιν ἐν σοὶ διὰ τῆς ἐπιθέσεως τῶν χειρῶν μου
verses.king_james: Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim dedit nobis Deus spiritum timoris: sed virtutis, et dilectionis, et sobrietatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ἔδωκεν ἡμῖν ὁ θεὸς πνεῦμα δειλίας ἀλλὰ δυνάμεως καὶ ἀγάπης καὶ σωφρονισμοῦ
verses.king_james: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli itaque erubescere testimonium Domini nostri, neque me vinctum ejus: sed collabora Evangelio secundum virtutem Dei:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ οὖν ἐπαισχυνθῇς τὸ μαρτύριον τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν μηδὲ ἐμὲ τὸν δέσμιον αὐτοῦ ἀλλὰ συγκακοπάθησον τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ κατὰ δύναμιν θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Be not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui nos liberavit, et vocavit vocatione sua sancta, non secundum opera nostra, sed secundum propositum suum, et gratiam, quæ data est nobis in Christo Jesu ante tempora sæcularia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ σώσαντος ἡμᾶς καὶ καλέσαντος κλήσει ἁγίᾳ οὐ κατὰ τὰ ἔργα ἡμῶν ἀλλὰ κατ᾽ ἰδίαν πρόθεσιν καὶ χάριν τὴν δοθεῖσαν ἡμῖν ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ πρὸ χρόνων αἰωνίων
verses.king_james: Who hath saved us, and called *us* with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began,

verses.latin_vulgate: Manifestata est autem nunc per illuminationem Salvatoris nostri Jesu Christi, qui destruxit quidem mortem, illuminavit autem vitam, et incorruptionem per Evangelium:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φανερωθεῖσαν δὲ νῦν διὰ τῆς ἐπιφανείας τοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ καταργήσαντος μὲν τὸν θάνατον φωτίσαντος δὲ ζωὴν καὶ ἀφθαρσίαν διὰ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου
verses.king_james: But is now made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel:

verses.latin_vulgate: in quo positus sum ego prædicator, et Apostolus, et magister gentium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς ὃ ἐτέθην ἐγὼ κῆρυξ καὶ ἀπόστολος καὶ διδάσκαλος ἐθνῶν
verses.king_james: Whereunto I am appointed a preacher, and an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ob quam causam etiam hæc patior, sed non confundor. Scio enim cui credidi, et certus sum quia potens est depositum meum servare in illum diem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δι᾽ ἣν αἰτίαν καὶ ταῦτα πάσχω ἀλλ᾽ οὐκ ἐπαισχύνομαι οἶδα γὰρ ᾧ πεπίστευκα καὶ πέπεισμαι ὅτι δυνατός ἐστιν τὴν παραθήκην μου φυλάξαι εἰς ἐκείνην τὴν ἡμέραν
verses.king_james: For the which cause I also suffer these things: nevertheless I am not ashamed: for I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed unto him against that day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Formam habe sanorum verborum, quæ a me audisti in fide, et in dilectione in Christo Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑποτύπωσιν ἔχε ὑγιαινόντων λόγων ὧν παρ᾽ ἐμοῦ ἤκουσας ἐν πίστει καὶ ἀγάπῃ τῇ ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: Hold fast the form of sound words, which thou hast heard of me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonum depositum custodi per Spiritum Sanctum, qui habitat in nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὴν καλὴν παρακαταθήκην φύλαξον διὰ πνεύματος ἁγίου τοῦ ἐνοικοῦντος ἐν ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: That good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the Holy Ghost which dwelleth in us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scis hoc, quod aversi sunt a me omnes, qui in Asia sunt, ex quibus est Phigellus, et Hermogenes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἶδας τοῦτο ὅτι ἀπεστράφησάν με πάντες οἱ ἐν τῇ ἀσίᾳ ὧν ἐστιν φύγελλος καὶ ἑρμογένης
verses.king_james: This thou knowest, that all they which are in Asia be turned away from me; of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Det misericordiam Dominus Onesiphori domui: quia sæpe me refrigeravit, et catenam meam non erubuit:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δῴη ἔλεος ὁ κύριος τῷ ὀνησιφόρου οἴκῳ ὅτι πολλάκις με ἀνέψυξεν καὶ τὴν ἅλυσίν μου οὐκ ἐπῆσχύνθη
verses.king_james: The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed cum Romam venisset, sollicite me quæsivit, et invenit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ γενόμενος ἐν ῥώμῃ σπουδαιότερον ἐζήτησέν με καὶ εὗρεν
verses.king_james: But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found *me*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Det illi Dominus invenire misericordiam a Domino in illa die. Et quanta Ephesi ministravit mihi, tu melius nosti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δῴη αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος εὑρεῖν ἔλεος παρὰ κυρίου ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ καὶ ὅσα ἐν ἐφέσῳ διηκόνησεν βέλτιον σὺ γινώσκεις
verses.king_james: The Lord grant unto him that he may find mercy of the Lord in that day: and in how many things he ministered unto me at Ephesus, thou knowest very well.
